generated_comment: Bro, half these senior women leaders in corporates are either diversity fast-tracks or they become so hyper-aggressive just to prove they belong. Then if you push back, suddenly you're the problem. Merit ka scene hi alag ho jata hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it undermines women leaders' merit by attributing their advancement to diversity policies and frames their leadership style as unnaturally aggressive. It encodes merit-undermining and the gendered double bind around women's competence in leadership.
persona_text: You are Nikhil. Age 32. Recently married three months ago. A product manager at a tech company in Bengaluru. You are on a late night call with your two best male friends from college after a frustrating day at work. Your female skip level manager just rejected your product roadmap proposal in front of the entire leadership team without giving you a chance to explain your reasoning. You are venting to your friends and telling them what happened and what you think of her as a leader.
